data.narrative:
Tomorrow.io is a Series E space-technology company that has raised $210M to date. Valuation benchmarking draws from a peer set of 4 private-stage comparables in Space & Aerospace (Astranis, ICEYE, Apex Space, Stoke Space) alongside 5 public-sector peers, anchoring analysis within a capital-intensive, pre-profitability sector.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. The stage-progression method, applying a 1.4x multiplier to the $210M total raised proxy, implies $294M. The peer-median approach yields $1.9B, derived from the median valuation across Astranis ($3.5B, Series D), ICEYE ($2.8B, Series E), Apex Space ($1.0B, Series D), and Stoke Space ($1.0B, Series D). The public-comparables approach cannot be applied: while the Space & Aerospace public sector trades at a median P/S of 64.2x across six tickers, Tomorrow.io's revenue is not disclosed. 

The two applicable methods produce a wide range: $294M (stage progression floor) to $1.9B (peer median). Triangulating these signals yields a valuation range of $250M–$2.2B, with a median point of $1.1B. Key uncertainties include the applicability of stage multipliers for frontier-tech firms, dispersion within the peer set ($1.0B–$3.5B), and whether undisclosed revenue would support higher multiples or justify stage-adjusted positioning.
